cases.title: Sequential registrations fill seats up to EventCapacity without overflow
preconditions: Event is RegistrationOpen with configured capacity N (e.g. N=5)
Zero existing Registered participants
Registration window is active
Each participant has no prior active registration for the event
steps: Register N distinct participants sequentially via registrationService.register
After each registration, query count of seat holders (Registered state) for the event
cases.expected: Each of the N registrations receives state Registered; after the Nth registration Registered count equals EventCapacity exactly; no registration assigns Registered when count would exceed capacity

cases.title: Concurrent POST registrations never produce more Registered than capacity
preconditions: Published event with registration opened, capacity 3, waitlistEnabled true
Ten or more distinct participant JWTs with no prior registration
Each request uses a unique Idempotency-Key
steps: Fire parallel POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations from all participants
Count response bodies with state Registered versus Waitlisted
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?state=Registered&page=1&pageSize=100 as organizer
cases.expected: Exactly capacity (3) responses have state Registered; remaining responses are Waitlisted or rejected per policy; organizer list total Registered count equals capacity and never exceeds it

cases.title: When event is full, new registrations do not increase Registered count beyond capacity
preconditions: Event capacity N with exactly N participants in Registered state
waitlistEnabled true
Registration window active
New participant with no active registration
steps: Register one additional participant via registrationService.register
Query Registered seat-holder count for the event
cases.expected: New registration has state Waitlisted (not Registered); Registered count remains N (equals EventCapacity); BR-03 invariant holds

cases.title: Capacity guard assigns Registered only when registeredCount is strictly less than capacity
preconditions: Pure capacity decision inputs: registeredCount, capacity, waitlistEnabled
steps: Evaluate capacity decision for registeredCount=2, capacity=5 → expect Registered
Evaluate for registeredCount=5, capacity=5, waitlistEnabled=true → expect Waitlisted
Evaluate for registeredCount=5, capacity=5, waitlistEnabled=false → expect rejection
cases.expected: Registered is chosen only when registeredCount < capacity; at equality the system never assigns another Registered seat

cases.title: Heavy concurrent burst (12+ attempts, capacity 3) never exceeds capacity
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with capacity 3 and waitlistEnabled true
12 or more unique participantIds with no prior registration
Parallel registrationService.register calls fired simultaneously
steps: Execute Promise.allSettled on all concurrent register calls
Query countSeatHolders (Registered) for the event in the database
cases.expected: Registered count is at most 3 and equals 3 when all attempts succeed; exactly (attempts - capacity) registrations are Waitlisted; zero overflow beyond EventCapacity under contention

cases.title: Cancellation and FIFO promotion keep Registered count at or below capacity
preconditions: Event capacity 2 with two Registered participants and one Waitlisted
waitlistEnabled true
One Registered participant cancels before CancellationDeadline (BR-07)
steps: Cancel one Registered registration to release a seat
Allow waitlist promotion to run atomically within the cancellation transaction
Query Registered seat-holder count
cases.expected: Registered count remains 2 (equals capacity); promoted participant moves from Waitlisted to Registered; total Registered never exceeds EventCapacity during or after the promotion

cases.title: Post-insert capacity guard rolls back transaction on CAPACITY_EXCEEDED
preconditions: Race condition or guard bypass attempt where insert would push Registered count above capacity
Registration module uses transactional BEGIN/COMMIT with post-insert count check
steps: Trigger registration path that inserts Registered then detects postCount > capacity
Observe error and database state after rollback
cases.expected: ApiError with code CAPACITY_EXCEEDED and HTTP 422; transaction is rolled back; no persisted Registered row causes count to exceed EventCapacity

cases.title: Capacity 1 boundary allows exactly one Registered participant
preconditions: Event capacity 1, RegistrationOpen, waitlistEnabled true
Three distinct participants with no prior registration
steps: Register first participant → expect Registered
Register second and third participants concurrently
Query Registered count
cases.expected: Exactly one Registered row exists at all times; second and third receive Waitlisted (or one succeeds as sole Registered under race); Registered count never exceeds 1

cases.title: Full event without waitlist rejects registration without consuming a Registered seat
preconditions: Event capacity 2 with two Registered participants
waitlistEnabled false
New participant with valid JWT
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations for the new participant
GET organizer registrations list filtered by state=Registered
cases.expected: HTTP 422 with REGISTRATION_REJECTED_FULL; Registered count remains 2 (at capacity, not above); no new Registered row created

cases.title: Waitlisted participants are excluded from Registered seat-holder count
preconditions: Event at capacity with multiple Waitlisted registrations
Registration window still open
steps: Query countSeatHolders (or equivalent Registered-only count)
Count total rows including Waitlisted state separately
cases.expected: Seat-holder count equals Registered rows only and equals EventCapacity; Waitlisted rows do not inflate Registered count toward or beyond capacity

cases.title: P0 invariant: Registered count never exceeds EventCapacity after any operation sequence
preconditions: Database with registration module transactional boundaries enabled
Mix of sequential and concurrent register, cancel, and promote operations on events with various capacities
steps: After each operation in the sequence, query Registered count and compare to event_rule_configs.capacity
Include Flow B scenario: full → waitlist → cancel → promote
cases.expected: registeredCount <= capacity holds after every commit; aligns with testing plan P0 severity for capacity overflow defects

cases.title: Registration assignment and capacity check execute in one atomic transaction
preconditions: Event with capacity 5
lockEventRuleConfig serializes concurrent writers per event
steps: Complete a successful registration that assigns Registered
Verify BEGIN/COMMIT wraps insert, post-insert count check, audit write, and waitlist enqueue (if any)
cases.expected: No intermediate state visible where Registered count exceeds capacity; failed capacity guard results in full ROLLBACK with no partial seat consumption

cases.title: Flow B lifecycle maintains capacity ceiling through cancel and promotion
preconditions: E2E Flow B fixture: full event (capacity 1), one Registered, one Waitlisted
Organizer and participant tokens available
steps: Execute Flow B: Registered participant cancels registration
Verify waitlisted participant is promoted to Registered
Query event Registered count via organizer API
cases.expected: After promotion exactly one Registered participant exists; count equals capacity (1) and never exceeds it at any step in the flow

cases.title: Organizer capacity meter reflects Registered count never above configured capacity
preconditions: Organizer logged in viewing event operations dashboard for an event at or near capacity
Event has known capacity N and N Registered participants (or fewer)
steps: Navigate to organizer event detail or registrations view showing capacity meter
Observe registered versus capacity display
Trigger additional participant registration (via API or second browser) up to capacity
cases.expected: UI capacity meter shows registered count less than or equal to capacity at all times; when full, display indicates no remaining seats without showing registered > capacity
